name: Schottky group — two generator example
description: A Schottky group with two generators, producing a Cantor-set limit set

construction.overview: Choose two pairs of disjoint circles (C1,C1') and (C2,C2') on the boundary. Define generators g1,g2 mapping the interior of C1 to the exterior of C1', etc.

g1.description: Maps exterior of C1 to interior of C1' (maps C1 onto C1')

g2.description: Maps exterior of C2 to interior of C2'

limitSet.description: The Cantor set intersection of all iterated preimage circles
limitSet.topology: Cantor set (perfect nowhere-dense closed set, uncountable, zero measure)
limitSet.fractalDimension: Hausdorff dimension between 0 and 1 (depends on group parameters)
limitSet.construction: Intersection of C(n) = g1^±1(C(n-1)) ∪ g2^±1(C(n-1)) for all n
limitSet.rendering: Count IFS iterations to convergence for each pixel; depth gives color

discontinuityRegion.description: The complement of the limit set where the group acts discontinuously
discontinuityRegion.structure: Infinitely many circular regions bounded by images of the Schottky circles

quotientsurface.description: The quotient manifold ℍ³/Γ is a genus-2 handlebody (Schottky 3-manifold)
quotientsurface.boundary: The quotient of the limit set gives the boundary Riemann surface
